meanings.definition: the work of making something smooth and shiny by rubbing or waxing it
examples: the shining of shoes provided a meager living
every Sunday he gave his car a good polishing

meanings.definition: be bright by reflecting or casting light
emit light; be bright, as of the sun or a light
examples: The sun shone bright that day
The fire beamed on their faces

meanings.definition: have a complexion with a strong bright color, such as red or pink
examples: Her face glowed when she came out of the sauna

meanings.definition: throw or flash the light of (a lamp)
examples: Shine the light on that window, please

meanings.definition: touch or seem as if touching visually or audibly
examples: The sun shone on the fields
The light struck the golden necklace
A strange sound struck my ears

meanings.definition: experience a feeling of well-being or happiness, as from good health or an intense emotion
climb awkwardly, as if by scrambling

examples: had shining virtues and few faults

meanings.definition: made smooth and bright by or as if by rubbing; reflecting a sheen or glow
examples: she brushed her hair until it fell in lustrous auburn waves
saw the moon like a shiny dime on a deep blue velvet carpet
